arbeit.label: Employment contract
arbeit.form: Signed written notice is the safest option; some employers accept email — check your contract
arbeit.tipp: Employment law differs considerably from country to country, and some jurisdictions require a handwritten signature on paper for a resignation to be valid. Check your employment contract and any staff handbook first, and if in doubt send a signed letter by recorded or registered post and keep proof of delivery. A short email confirming that the signed letter is on its way is a useful backup, not a replacement.

miete.label: Home / tenancy agreement
miete.form: Signed written notice is normally expected; email alone is often not sufficient — check your tenancy agreement
miete.tipp: Tenancy law is strongly national and often protective of tenants, including strict rules on how notice must be given. Read the notice clause in your tenancy agreement and check the rules that apply where the property is located. In practice a signed letter sent by recorded or registered post, with a copy kept for your records, is the safest route.

handy.label: Mobile / internet / phone
handy.form: Written notice; many providers also accept email or an online cancellation function — check your contract
handy.tipp: Most telecoms providers set out an accepted cancellation channel in their terms and conditions, and many now offer an online cancellation option or a dedicated cancellation address. Follow the channel named in your contract, and keep the confirmation email, reference number or screenshot as proof. If no channel is named, send written notice and ask for written confirmation of the end date.

strom.label: Electricity / gas
strom.form: Written notice; many suppliers also accept email or an online account request — check your contract
strom.tipp: Energy suppliers usually describe the cancellation or switching procedure in their terms and conditions, and in many markets a switch is handled by the new supplier on your behalf. Check your contract and your local rules before sending anything, and note that supply contracts often have a separate deregistration step when you move. Always record your final meter readings and keep written confirmation.

streaming.label: Streaming / subscription
streaming.form: Written notice; most providers also accept cancellation in the account settings or by email — check your contract
streaming.tipp: Subscriptions taken out online can usually be cancelled online, and that is generally the quickest and best-documented route. Check the terms you accepted at sign-up for the cancellation method and any minimum term. Take a screenshot of the confirmation and check your next statement to make sure billing has actually stopped.

fitness.label: Gym / fitness membership
fitness.form: Written notice; some operators also accept email — check your membership agreement
fitness.tipp: Gym memberships often combine a minimum term with an automatic renewal clause, so the cancellation deadline matters as much as the method. Read your membership agreement carefully and send your notice well before the renewal date. If the agreement does not clearly permit email, send a signed letter by recorded or registered post and keep proof.

versicherung.label: Insurance
versicherung.form: Written notice; requirements vary between insurers and jurisdictions — check your policy documents
versicherung.tipp: Insurance policies frequently set out their own notice requirements, including the permitted method and any cooling-off or renewal windows. Read your policy documents and any renewal notice before cancelling, and make sure you have replacement cover in place where cover is legally required. If the permitted method is unclear, written notice sent by recorded or registered post is the safer choice.

verein.label: Club / association membership
verein.form: As set out in the club's rules, statutes or constitution
verein.tipp: Clubs and associations are usually governed by their own statutes or rules, which may specify who must receive the notice, in what form and by what date. Ask for a copy of the rules if you do not have one, and address your notice to the person or office named there. If nothing is specified, send written notice and request written acknowledgement.

sonstiges.label: Other contract
sonstiges.form: Depends on the contract; written notice is the safest default — check your terms and conditions
sonstiges.tipp: Where the contract type is not covered above, the terms and conditions you agreed to are the first place to look, followed by the consumer rules that apply where you live. If the contract is silent or ambiguous, send written notice and ask for written confirmation of receipt and of the end date. Keeping proof of delivery costs little and settles most later disputes.

miet-kuend.body:
I am writing to give notice to end the tenancy for the property at {objekt}, in accordance with the notice period stated in my tenancy agreement.

Requested end of tenancy: {datum}

If notice to that date is not possible, I ask you to end the tenancy at the earliest date permitted under the agreement and to let me know that date.

Please confirm receipt of this notice and the end date of the tenancy in writing. I would also like to arrange a joint handover inspection of the property, and I ask you to let me know how and when the deposit will be returned.

nachsende.titel: Redirecting my mail to a new address

uebergabe.body:
Checklist for handing back a rented property:

- Record all meter readings (electricity, gas, water) and photograph them
- Hand over every key and record how many of each type were returned
- Leave the property clean and clear of your belongings
- Check walls, ceilings and floors for damage
- Fill and make good any drill holes, where your agreement requires it
- Check that windows, doors and fittings work properly
- Complete and sign a joint handover record with the landlord or agent
- Provide bank details for the return of the deposit
- Take dated photographs of the condition of every room
- Arrange redirection of your post to your new address
- Confirm the final billing date with your utility providers

maengel.titel: Reporting a defect to the landlord

kaution.titel: Requesting the return of a tenancy deposit

internet-kuend.titel: Cancelling an internet or broadband contract

adress-behoerde.titel: Notifying an authority of a new address

aenderungsmitteilung.titel: Notifying an authority of a change in circumstances
aenderungsmitteilung.body:
I am writing to notify you of the following change in my personal circumstances:

{text}

Please update your records accordingly and take this change into account in any ongoing matter or claim. If any further information, form or supporting document is required, please let me know.

Please confirm receipt of this notification.

kindergeld.titel: Notifying a change relating to child benefit or family payments

steuer-adress.titel: Notifying the tax authority of a new address

eigenkuendigung.body:
I am writing to give notice to terminate my employment, in accordance with the notice period stated in my contract of employment.

Requested last day of employment: {datum}

If my notice period means that a different date applies, please treat this as notice for the earliest date permitted under my contract and confirm that date to me.

Please confirm receipt of this letter and my final working day in writing. I would also be grateful for a written reference covering my role and performance, together with a statement of any outstanding holiday entitlement and final pay. I will of course do what I can to ensure a smooth handover of my work.

arbeit-bank.titel: Changing bank details for salary payments

arbeitszeugnis.body:
My employment with you has now ended.

Last day of employment: {datum}

I would be grateful if you could provide me with a written reference covering my role, my responsibilities and my performance and conduct during my time with the company.

Please send the reference to the address given above, or by email if that is more convenient. If your policy is to provide references only in a standard format or only on request from a future employer, please let me know so that I can pass on the correct contact details. Thank you.

reklamation.titel: Disputing a payment taken from my account

strom.titel: Transferring an electricity contract to a new address

gas.titel: Transferring a gas contract to a new address

wasser.titel: Transferring a water account to a new address

muell.titel: Updating waste collection charges after a move

rundfunk.titel: Updating a public broadcasting fee or licence after a move

halterwechsel.titel: Notifying a change of registered keeper

adress-freunde.titel: Telling friends and family about a new address

namens-mitteilen.titel: Telling friends and family about a change of name

vollmacht.titel: Authorisation to act on my behalf

frei.body:
{text}

I would be grateful for your written confirmation that you have received this letter, and for a reply setting out how you intend to deal with the matter. If you need any further information from me, please let me know.

hinweise: nachsende: the German 'Nachsendeauftrag' of Deutsche Post has been generalised to a neutral request for mail redirection addressed to any postal operator, with an added sentence asking which form, ID or fee is required.
kindergeld: 'Kindergeld/Elterngeld' has been generalised to 'child benefit or family-related payments' without naming any agency, benefit scheme or reference-number format.

steuer-adress: the German 'Steuernummer' and Finanzamt structure has been generalised to 'the tax authority' and a neutral tax reference number; a sentence was added asking whether a specific form or online service must be used.
muell: 'Müllgebühren' has been generalised to 'waste collection and refuse charges', which may be billed by a municipality, a utility or a private contractor depending on the country.

rundfunk: 'Rundfunkbeitrag' has been generalised to 'public broadcasting fee or licence'; several countries (including Ireland's TV licence and the UK licence fee) have comparable but differently structured schemes, and some have none at all.
elternzeit: German statutory 'Elternzeit' has been generalised to a request for parental leave under the contract and applicable local rules; no statutory duration or entitlement is asserted.

lastschrift: the SEPA direct debit mandate has been generalised to 'direct debit mandate or recurring payment authorisation'; an explicit sentence was added clarifying that cancelling the mandate does not terminate the underlying contract.

fahrzeug-anab and halterwechsel: German vehicle registration (Zulassungsstelle, Halter) has been generalised to a neutral request to the vehicle registration authority; the letters ask which documents and fees apply rather than assuming a procedure. Both now use {kennzeichen} on a labelled line, since a registration number is not formatted the same way in every country.

vollmacht: kept as a general authorisation to act, now using {name}, {bevoll} and {zweck}, with an express warning that formal or notarial requirements vary and that banks, authorities and courts often require their own form.

kaution: the German 14-day payment deadline was removed and replaced with a request for an itemised statement of any deductions, since deposit-protection rules and repayment deadlines differ widely (for example statutory deposit schemes in the UK and Ireland).

uebergabe is a checklist rather than a letter; it has been rendered as a plain bullet list in the body field and the German 'besenrein' standard was replaced with a neutral 'clean and clear of your belongings'.

Placeholder discipline: every body was checked against that template's own `fields` list in the source file. No body contains a placeholder the template does not offer. {name} is used in kaution, arbeit-bank, fin-bank and vollmacht, where the German original inserted the sender name via p.name; {absAdresse} and {betreffFrei} are available but not needed in any body.

Optional fields are rendered on their own labelled line ('Policy number: {vertragsnr}', 'Amount: {betrag}', 'Date of death: {sterbedatum}') rather than inline in a sentence. This is a change from the previous version: with generic raw substitution an inline 'my contract{vertragsnr}' would render as 'my contract12345', whereas a labelled line stays grammatical whether the value is present or an empty string.

Date handling: {datum} always appears on a labelled line ('Requested end date: {datum}', 'Effective from: {datum}'). That reads correctly with a formatted date, with the fallback wording 'at the earliest possible date', and with an empty string, so it is safe under either substitution behaviour.

Corrections to the previous version: elternzeit and urlaub used {datum}, which is not in their fields — they now use {von} and {bis}. todesfall routed the name of the deceased through {text} and now uses {verstorben} and {sterbedatum}, with {text} reserved for the optional additional words the form actually offers. vollmacht routed the authorised person and the purpose through {text} and now uses {bevoll} and {zweck}. lastschrift, reklamation and ratenzahlung now use {betrag}; kfz-vers-kuend, fahrzeug-anab and halterwechsel now use {kennzeichen}.

No national statutes are cited anywhere, and no concrete notice period is asserted; all termination letters refer to 'the notice period stated in my contract' and include a fallback request for the earliest permitted date.
unsicher: Employment termination form requirements: several jurisdictions require a handwritten signature on paper for a resignation to be effective, others accept email. The 'arbeit' entry is deliberately set to 'unsicher'; a local employment lawyer should confirm the wording used in each launch country.

Tenancy notice: notice periods, permitted service methods and deposit-return deadlines are strongly national (statutory deposit protection schemes in the UK and Ireland, rent-pressure rules, etc.). Both miet-kuend and kaution should be reviewed per country.

kaution / fin-bank / arbeit-bank: the {iban} field is labelled 'IBAN / account number' because IBAN is not used in every target market (the UK uses sort code and account number). If the UK is a launch market, a separate field or label may be needed.
{betrag} is a free-text field, so the user supplies the currency. The letters never name a currency themselves; check that the field placeholder shown in the English UI does not suggest euro amounts.

reklamation: chargeback and payment-dispute rights, and the applicable time limits, differ between jurisdictions and between card schemes and bank transfers. The letter deliberately makes no claim about a right to a refund and instead asks for a review.

vollmacht: the highest-risk template. In many jurisdictions a private written authorisation will be refused by banks, land registries, hospitals and courts. Local legal review is strongly recommended, and consider adding an in-app warning.

todesfall: probate and estate notification requirements are entirely national; this template is written purely as a personal announcement to friends and family and should not be presented as sufficient notification to any institution.

elternzeit: entitlement, minimum notice and the form of the application vary greatly. Both {von} and {bis} are optional in the form, so the letter is phrased to work as an opening request even if only one date is given.
kfz-vers-kuend: the compulsory-insurance warning is worded generally ('in most countries'); confirm that this is accurate for each launch market.
rundfunk: some target countries have no public broadcasting fee at all, so this template may be better hidden per country rather than shown with a generalised text.

Rendering of empty optional fields: labelled lines keep the grammar intact, but an empty value leaves a dangling label such as 'Amount:'. Consider having the app drop an entire line when its only placeholder resolves to an empty string, and collapse the resulting blank paragraphs — that would make these templates read perfectly in every combination. This affects the optional free-text fields too ({text} in ratenzahlung, todesfall).

General: the entire set is written as orientation for the user, not as legal advice. A short disclaimer to that effect should be displayed alongside the English templates, mirroring KUENDIGUNG_DISCLAIMER in the German version.
